title: Canon Senses
description: The homonym-split sidecar, sibling of canon.json (default path {durable_root}/canon_senses.json, --senses-path override). Records a source_form that resolves to TWO OR MORE distinct target senses within one project (a split is >=2 senses -- a would-be 1-sense record is rejected outright, never treated as 'not split'). Read exclusively through canon_senses.py::load_senses, which schema-validates against THIS file first and then applies three procedural rejects this schema cannot express: a duplicate sense_id within one entry (uniqueItems sees distinct objects as distinct, so it cannot catch this), a non-NFC entries_by_source_form key, and two keys colliding under the shared normalize_form() comparator (NFC+casefold+whitespace-collapse). Evidence is the MINIMAL VERIFIABLE SET only -- a matcher-authenticated occurrence span plus an enclosing context window and its raw-byte sha256; the derived 'quote'/'context_window' strings are computed from those offsets at READ time (evidence_verify.py / any renderer) and must never be stored here, so this schema deliberately has no properties for them and additionalProperties:false rejects them if present. Strict at every level: additionalProperties:false and a full required[] at every object, so an authoring or codex-workflow bug (a stray stored quote, a 1-sense record, a missing evidence field) fails LOUD at load time rather than silently under-enforcing the mandatory evidence gate (SKILL.md's W-step).

entries_by_source_form.description: Keyed by the entry's own NFC-normalized source_form (enforced procedurally by load_senses, not expressible in JSON Schema). An empty object here is the schema-valid EMPTY state load_senses distinguishes from a genuinely-absent file.

senses.description: A split is >=2 senses -- minItems enforces that a 1-sense record is a hard schema-validation failure, never a silent 'not split'.

sense_id.description: Unique within this entry's senses[] -- non-blank, but uniqueItems cannot catch a duplicate sense_id across two otherwise-distinct sense objects, so load_senses additionally rejects duplicates procedurally.

disambiguator.description: Human-readable label distinguishing this sense from its siblings (e.g. the referent's short gloss).

block.description: Manifest block id this sense's occurrence was found in (e.g. "PARA:seg02:0001").

seg.description: Owning segment id, or null for a block not (yet) assigned to any segment -- mirrors manifest.schema.json's own blocks{}.seg.

char_end.description: Half-open codepoint offset: the occurrence span is [char_start, char_end). Matcher-authenticated by evidence_verify.py against production_occurrences(), never a mere in-bounds substring.

context_end.description: Half-open codepoint offset of the enclosing context window, which must contain [char_start, char_end).

sha256.description: sha256 of the exact raw UTF-8 bytes of [context_start, context_end) -- NOT NFC-normalized -- as verified by evidence_verify.py.
